mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to future earnings and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when the policy rate is falling on a 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not above their unconditional baseline during periods when FEDFUNDS is down versus 3 months earlier, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above a local trend, the market anticipates tighter real policy and lower valuation multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days are negative after the inflation breakout.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if post-breakout 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered cases or if the average triggered return is not below zero across any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE paired with falling PAYEMS signals weakening labor demand and slower household income growth, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-trigger sample does not show negative mean 20-day SPX returns, or if either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail spending and revenue momentum, which supports near-term earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of positive RSAFS trend do not coincide with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year relation turns persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which raises earnings revisions for cyclicals and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO does not precede positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the relation fails in more than two consecutive 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while GDPC1 is still expanding, the housing-sensitive part of the economy is losing momentum before the broader economy, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days turn negative as growth expectations weaken.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines below its 6-month moving average while GDPC1 remains positive year over year
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-growth signal does not produce below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once GDPC1 is excluded.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series is a direct signal of weakening payroll growth, and when payrolls contract over several months the market discounts slower consumer income and weaker earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative or underperforming 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips consistently after controlling for broader market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, a stable or falling CPIAUCSL combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5 percent signals disinflation without further tightening, which should support equity multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is flat or declining
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-rate/disinflation state does not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is weaker than either series alone in rolling out-of-sample tests.
